docs.title: Attributes

issues.suggestion: Change the description of `number` from "`number` (same as `integer`)" to "`number` (accepts any numeric value; note: unlike JSON Schema, `integer` and `number` use the same runtime check of `typeof value === 'number'`)". This clarifies that while JSON Schema treats `integer` as a subset of `number` (whole numbers only), this implementation does not enforce that distinction — both pass the same `typeof value === 'number'` check, meaning a float like `1.5` would pass the `integer` type check.

positives: The documentation correctly describes the `role` property with its two valid values (`content` and `local`), matching both the JSON schema enum `["content", "local"]` and the serializer's handling (`if ( attributeSchema.role === 'local' )`).

The documentation accurately describes the `meta` source as deprecated and correctly points to EntityProvider and related hook APIs as the recommended alternative, consistent with the schema's description: "meta attribute sources are considered deprecated; EntityProvider and related hook APIs should be used instead".

The documentation correctly explains that attributes without a `source` are saved in the block comment delimiter, which matches the serializer code: `// Ignore all attributes but the ones with an "undefined" source / "undefined" source refers to attributes saved in the block comment.`

docs.title: Bindings

evidence.docSays: `label`: `string` with the human-readable name of the custom source.

issues.suggestion: In the documentation's `$source_properties` parameter table, mark `label` as **Required** (the same way `get_value_callback` should be marked). The PHP registry explicitly checks for its presence and returns `false` if it is absent, making `label` a required field — not an implicitly optional one. The PHPDoc in `class-wp-block-bindings-registry.php` around line 68 (`@type string $label The label of the source.`) also lacks the `Required.` prefix that WordPress convention uses (contrast with `uses_context` which is correctly marked `Optional.`), so both the user-facing docs and the source PHPDoc block should be updated to add `Required.` before the `label` and `get_value_callback` descriptions.

positives: The documentation correctly identifies all three parameters of the `get_value_callback` (`source_args`, `block_instance`, `attribute_name`), matching the PHP registry's documented callback signature exactly.

The `block_bindings_source_value` filter parameters (`value`, `name`, `source_args`, `block_instance`, `attribute_name`) match those defined in `WP_Block_Bindings_Source::get_value()` in class-wp-block-bindings-source.php.

The JS `registerBlockBindingsSource` destructured properties (`name`, `label`, `usesContext`, `getValues`, `setValues`, `canUserEditValue`, `getFieldsList`) accurately match the source code in packages/blocks/src/api/registration.ts.

docs.title: Context
positives: The documentation correctly states that block context is available from the `context` property of the `$block` argument (a `WP_Block` instance) passed as the third argument to `render_callback`, which matches the `call_user_func( $this->block_type->render_callback, $this->attributes, $block_content, $this )` call in `WP_Block::render()`.

The documentation correctly describes `providesContext` as a property mapping context names to block attribute names, which matches the PHP implementation in `WP_Block::refresh_parsed_block_dependents()` where `foreach ( $this->block_type->provides_context as $context_name => $attribute_name )` is used to populate child context.

The documentation correctly states that `usesContext` should be assigned as an array of context names, which matches the PHP implementation in `WP_Block::refresh_context_dependents()` where `foreach ( $this->block_type->uses_context as $context_name )` iterates over the array.

docs.title: Deprecation

evidence.docSays: This function is **not** called when the results of all previous deprecations' save functions were invalid.

issues.suggestion: Replace the sentence "This function is **not** called when the results of all previous deprecations' save functions were invalid." with: "This function is only consulted when `block.isValid` is `true`. When the block is currently invalid (i.e. the current `save` output did not match), `isEligible` is **not** called and the deprecation is attempted unconditionally. In other words, `isEligible` acts as an opt-in gate only for *valid* blocks that should still be migrated; it does not gate invalid blocks at all."

positives: The documentation correctly describes the `isEligible` function signature with three parameters (`attributes`, `innerBlocks`, `data`) including the `data.blockNode` and `data.block` sub-properties, which matches the call in `apply-block-deprecated-versions.ts`: `isEligible(parsedAttributes, block.innerBlocks, { blockNode: rawBlock, block })`.

The documentation accurately describes `migrate`'s return value as 'Either the updated block attributes or tuple array `[attributes, innerBlocks]`', which matches the code's handling: `if ( ! Array.isArray( migrated ) ) { migrated = [ migrated ] ... } [ migratedAttributes = parsedAttributes, migratedInnerBlocks = block.innerBlocks ] = migrated;`

The documentation correctly states that `attributes`, `supports`, and `save` are not automatically inherited from the current version, which matches the code using `DEPRECATED_ENTRY_KEYS` to omit those properties from the blockType before merging the deprecated definition: `omit(blockType, DEPRECATED_ENTRY_KEYS)`.

docs.title: Metadata in block.json

issues.suggestion: Change the `apiVersion` property listing from `Optional` to `Required` in the block metadata documentation, since the JSON schema's top-level `required` array at the end of `schemas/json/block.json` explicitly includes `"apiVersion"`. Additionally, the schema constrains the type to `"integer"` (not `number`) with `"const": 3`, so the type should be updated to reflect that only the integer value `3` is currently valid.

evidence.docSays: **Note:** A block name can only contain lowercase alphanumeric characters, dashes, and at most one forward slash to designate the plugin-unique namespace prefix. It must begin with a letter.

issues.suggestion: In `block-metadata.md`, replace the note with: "A block name must follow the format `namespace/block-name`. Both the namespace and the block-name segments may only contain lowercase alphanumeric characters and dashes, and each segment must begin with a letter (matching `^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*/[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$`)." The current wording "It must begin with a letter" implies only the full string needs to start with a letter, but the regex enforces that **both** segments independently begin with a letter.

positives: The documentation correctly identifies `name` and `title` as Required properties, which is confirmed by the JSON schema's `"required": [ "apiVersion", "name", "title" ]`.
The `blockHooks` allowed position values ('before', 'after', 'firstChild', 'lastChild') described in prose match the schema's `enum: [ "before", "after", "firstChild", "lastChild" ]` exactly.

The `variations` type documented as `object[]|WPDefinedPath` correctly reflects the JSON schema's `oneOf` allowing either a string (PHP file path) or an array of variation objects, consistent with the schema's `"oneOf": [ { "type": "string" }, { "type": "array", ... } ]`.

docs.title: Patterns

evidence.codeSays: @type string   $filePath      Optional. The full path to the file containing the block pattern content.

issues.suggestion: Add `filePath` (optional, added in 6.5.0) to the documented list of `register_block_pattern` properties, describing it as "The full path to the file containing the block pattern content. Can be used instead of `content`; if neither `content` nor `filePath` is set, the pattern will not be registered."

positives: The documentation correctly identifies `title` as a required property for `register_block_pattern`, which matches the source code (`@type string $title Required. A human-readable title for the pattern.`).

The documentation accurately describes `inserter` (optional) with the correct behavior: "To hide a pattern so that it can only be inserted programmatically, set the `inserter` to `false`", which matches the source code (`@type bool $inserter Optional. Determines whether the pattern is visible in inserter. To hide a pattern so that it can only be inserted programmatically, set this to false. Default true.`).

The documentation correctly describes `blockTypes` and the template-part area specificity (e.g., `core/template-part/header`), which matches the source code note: "Certain blocks support further specificity besides the block name (e.g. for `core/template-part` you can specify areas like `core/template-part/header` or `core/template-part/footer`).".

docs.title: Registration

evidence.docSays:
#### Icon

-   **Type:** `Object`

(Optional) An icon to display alongside the title in the block inserter.
evidence.codeSays: export type Icon = string | ReactElement | ComponentType;

issues.suggestion: Change the `icon` type description under `registerBlockCollection` settings from `Object` to `String | Element | Component` to match the `Icon` type definition (`string | ReactElement | ComponentType`) in `packages/blocks/src/types.ts`.

evidence.docSays:
The core provided categories are:

-   text
-   media
-   design
-   widgets
-   theme
-   embed
evidence.codeSays:
{ slug: 'text', title: __( 'Text' ) },
	{ slug: 'media', title: __( 'Media' ) },
	{ slug: 'design', title: __( 'Design' ) },
	{ slug: 'widgets', title: __( 'Widgets' ) },
	{ slug: 'theme', title: __( 'Theme' ) },
	{ slug: 'embed', title: __( 'Embeds' ) },
	{ slug: 'reusable', title: __( 'Reusable blocks' ) },

issues.suggestion: Add `reusable` to the list of core provided categories in the `### Category` section of `block-metadata.md`. The `DEFAULT_CATEGORIES` array in `packages/blocks/src/store/reducer.ts` includes `{ slug: 'reusable', title: __( 'Reusable blocks' ) }` as a seventh entry that is entirely absent from the documentation.

positives: The `registerBlockType` function signature is accurately documented as taking a block name string and a settings object, matching the TypeScript overloads in registration.ts.

The `registerBlockCollection` function is correctly documented as taking `namespace` (string) plus `{ title, icon }` settings, accurately matching the source signature `registerBlockCollection(namespace: string, { title, icon }: { title: string; icon?: Icon })`.
The `blockHooks` allowed target values (`before`, `after`, `firstChild`, `lastChild`) are correctly documented and match the `$position_mappings` array in blocks.php.

docs.title: Supports

evidence.docSays: When the block declares support for `color.gradient`, its attributes definition is extended to include two new attributes: gradient and style

issues.suggestion: The support property key is `gradients` (plural) in both the JSON schema property name and the `hasGradientSupport()` runtime check (`colorSupport.gradients`). All documentation references to `color.gradient` (singular) as the support key should be updated to `color.gradients`. Specifically, the section heading and any inline references that say `color.gradient` should be changed to `color.gradients`.

evidence.docSays:
- Subproperties:
    -   `height`: type `boolean`, default value `false`
    -   `minHeight`: type `boolean`, default value `false`
    -   `minWidth`: type `boolean`, default value `false`
    -   `width`: type `boolean`, default value `false`

issues.suggestion: Add `aspectRatio` as a subproperty of `dimensions` in the documentation's subproperties list. It should be documented with type `boolean` and default value `false`, matching the entry in `schemas/json/block.json` and the `hasDimensionsSupport` check in `packages/block-editor/src/hooks/dimensions.js`.

positives: The documentation correctly describes `color.gradients` as sourcing presets from `editor-gradient-presets` theme support, and the code in color.js correctly checks `colorSupport.gradients` (plural) — the only inaccuracy is that the documentation refers to the *support key name* as `color.gradient` (singular) rather than `color.gradients` in several prose paragraphs, while elsewhere accurately uses the plural form.

The documentation accurately describes `get_block_wrapper_attributes()` accepting an optional `$extra_attributes` array parameter, which matches the function signature in `class-wp-block-supports.php`: `function get_block_wrapper_attributes( $extra_attributes = array() )`.

The documentation correctly describes the `interactivity` support as accepting both a `boolean` and an `object` with `clientNavigation` and `interactive` sub-properties, which matches the PHP parsing logic in `blocks.php`: `$supports_interactivity_true = isset( $metadata['supports']['interactivity'] ) && true === $metadata['supports']['interactivity']` and the individual `interactive` / `clientNavigation` checks.

docs.title: Transforms

evidence.docSays: The schemas are passed into [`cleanNodeList` from `@wordpress/dom`](https://github.com/wordpress/gutenberg/blob/trunk/packages/dom/src/dom/clean-node-list.js); check there for a [complete description of the schema](https://github.com/wordpress/gutenberg/blob/trunk/packages/dom/src/phrasing-content.js).

issues.suggestion: In the 'Schemas and Content Models' section, replace any reference to `wp.blocks.getPhrasingContentSchema` (or the `@wordpress/blocks` export of `getPhrasingContentSchema`) with `wp.dom.getPhrasingContentSchema` from `@wordpress/dom`. The `@wordpress/blocks` version has been deprecated since WordPress 5.6 — the wrapper function `deprecatedGetPhrasingContentSchema` in `packages/blocks/src/api/raw-handling/index.ts` now simply delegates to `getPhrasingContentSchema` from `@wordpress/dom` after logging a deprecation warning.

positives: The documentation correctly describes the `isMultiBlock` parameter behavior: 'if true, the `transform` function's first parameter will be an array containing each selected block's attributes, and the second an array of each selected block's inner blocks' — this matches `factory.ts` where `transformation.transform!(blocksArray.map((currentBlock) => currentBlock.attributes), blocksArray.map((currentBlock) => currentBlock.innerBlocks))` is called when `transformation.isMultiBlock` is true.

The documentation accurately describes the `findTransform` priority system: 'a lower value will take precedence over higher values... the default priority is `10`' — this matches `factory.ts` where `hooks.addFilter` uses `candidate.priority` directly, leveraging the WordPress hooks library priority queue which defaults to 10.

The documentation correctly describes the `ungroup` transform as a callback that 'receives the attributes and inner blocks of the block being processed' and 'should return an array of block objects', which is consistent with the `transforms` key handling pattern throughout `factory.ts`.

docs.title: Variations

evidence.docSays: The Editor respects the first registered variation with `isDefault`, which might not be yours.

issues.suggestion: In the 'Caveats to using `isDefault`' section, replace 'The Editor respects the first registered variation with `isDefault`' with 'The Editor respects the **last** registered variation with `isDefault`'. The `getDefaultBlockVariation` selector reverses the variations array before searching, so the last registered variation with `isDefault: true` wins, not the first.

positives: The documentation correctly describes that `scope` defaults to `['block', 'inserter']` at runtime when not set, which is confirmed by the selector code: `return ( variation.scope || [ 'block', 'inserter' ] ).includes( scope )`.

The PHP filter hook name `get_block_type_variations` and its two parameters (`$variations` array and `$block_type` WP_Block_Type object) are accurately documented, matching the `apply_filters( 'get_block_type_variations', $this->variations, $this )` call in class-wp-block-type.php.

The documentation's description of `isActive` string-array specificity (choosing the variation with the most matched attributes) is accurately reflected in the selector code: `if ( isMatch && definedAttributesLength > maxMatchedAttributes ) { match = variation; maxMatchedAttributes = definedAttributesLength; }`.
